title: Internal Anchor Text Guidelines
content_md:
# Internal Anchor Text Guidelines

- Code: 892b004d
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=892b004d3b5d46448803e50528ca0536
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/251a92f6c1f644c5aeb153847be62972?sid=c03c4ce5-421c-4011-966c-0edaa4fcc20c?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 2m 28s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So let's talk about internal anchor text
So this is when you have links on your website from one URL one page of your website to a different URL
What anchor text should you use so we just have some general guidelines number one?
We don't we do not want to repeat any anchor text every time we use anchor text
That should be unique do not repeat anything number two. We want to incorporate keywords
But we want to do it naturally. We don't want it to look like we're keyword stuffing number three
We want the anchor text to be descriptive of the URL we're linking to of the destination URL
We don't want to describe the URL. They're on we want to describe the URL
They'd be going to if they clicked on the link, okay number four
We want to use descriptive text and number five. They should be in the body text
They should not be in headings
They shouldn't be in the footer if we can avoid it or in the nav bar or in the side
They should be in the body text in the last general guideline is to avoid branding
You should not in general be using your brand in your internal links
so let me go through some examples of good anchor text and
We're keeping with our same
motorized pergola keyword that we're going to give some examples with so learn more about choosing the right materials for pergolas
That's a good one very descriptive
It's using the keyword pergola, but it's not keyword stuffing pergola material options again
That's a good example benefits of motorized pergolas
The person who clicks on that link and Google more importantly knows exactly what they're going to find when they go there
Okay, and the last one here installation tips for motorized pergolas
Clearly we would want that to go to a URL with detailed installation tips. Okay, let's talk about some bad examples
Okay, click here. That's too generic. We don't want to use anything like that
We also don't use anything too long right here's a detailed guide on the various material options
You can choose for building a pergola is way too long. We want to be concise
Okay, and then we like if motorized pergola was the target keyword
We do not want to create 15 links that all use motorized pergolas and point back to it, right?
That's over optimization that will hurt your ranking like we said in general guidelines only use it once and then clearly the fourth bad example
installation tips for motorized pergolas is a terrible anchor text if the link is going to a URL about general home improvement
Okay, so hopefully this helps you with internal anchor text. Don't overthink it
Don't worry about it too much. Just don't just keep it natural
Okay, that's the best advice that I can give is just give keep the internal anchor text natural

summary: This lesson explains how to use internal anchor text effectively on a website. It advises against repeating anchor text, using keywords naturally, making descriptive links, avoiding branding, and keeping the text concise and natural.
